content_evals.prompt: Review PR #7. The diff adds this in app/api/products/route.ts line 42: const result = await db.query(`SELECT * FROM products WHERE id = '${id}'`)
content_evals.expected_without_skill: Likely a vague comment about SQL injection with no file/line reference and no structured verdict table
content_evals.delta: Structured BLOCKER finding at exact file:line vs informal comment

content_evals.prompt: Review PR #12. The diff adds `import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'` at the top of lib/orders.ts (line 1).
content_evals.expected_without_skill: May or may not catch; no consistent structure; no guideline rule reference
content_evals.delta: Guideline rule-cited structured finding vs informal or missed

content_evals.description: Missing JSDoc on new exported function
content_evals.prompt: Review PR #3. The diff adds `export async function applyPromoCode(db: Db, code: string): Promise<number>` in lib/promo.ts at line 14 with no JSDoc comment above it.
content_evals.expected_without_skill: May miss entirely or give informal comment without line reference or consistent output format
content_evals.delta: Consistent structured output with file:line vs ad-hoc or missed

trigger_evals.description: ~20 queries split 60/40 train/val. Val gate: >=50% should-trigger correct AND <50% should-not-trigger fired.
should_trigger_train: is this PR ready to merge?
can you review the diff in PR #5?
does this PR follow our team guidelines?
look at PR #8 and tell me if it is good to ship
should_trigger_val: check this change for security issues
does PR #12 respect our architecture principles?
I opened a PR, can you review it?
review the code changes in this PR against our standards
should_not_trigger_train: how do I create a pull request?
what is the diff between main and my branch?
run the tests for this branch
fix the linting errors in my code
should_not_trigger_val: write unit tests for this function
update the README with the new API
